position: First 2 bytes of the request packet
explanation: Numeric operation code that identifies the TFTP packet type and thus the request operation (e.g., Read vs Write). The opcode determines how the server will respond (e.g., a RRQ typically leads to DATA/OACK/ERROR; a WRQ to ACK/OACK/ERROR).

position: NULL-terminated string immediately following the filename in RRQ/WRQ
explanation: Textual transfer mode that identifies the transfer subtype/semantics for the request (affects data interpretation/translation and handling).

position: NULL-terminated option name strings appended after the mode in RRQ/WRQ (opt1..optN)
explanation: Case-insensitive option identifiers requested by the client to negotiate transfer parameters; the server distinguishes which options to acknowledge (via OACK) versus omit, thereby affecting the negotiated transfer behavior.
